claude-code-flow.name: ui-token-extractor
claude-code-flow.description: Extracts design tokens from itinerary app screenshots

files.content:
export const TOKEN_EXTRACTION_PROMPT = `
ANALYZE THIS SCREENSHOT using our naming scheme:

PRIMITIVE:
• color.primitive.gray.50 → #F9FAFB
• space.primitive.16 → 16px
• radius.primitive.12 → 12px

SEMANTIC:
• color.background.primary → color.primitive.gray.50
• space.card.padding → space.primitive.16
• color.action.primary.default → color.primitive.green.500

ACCESSIBILITY:
• accessibility.contrast.primary-text-on-surface → 4.8:1
• accessibility.tap-target.minimum → 44x44px

Extract ALL tokens as valid JSON: {colors: {}, spacing: {}, ...}
`;

export const COMPONENT_PROMPT = `
Identify components: list row (image+title+subtitle), day header, primary button (radius.24), drag handle.
Document states: default/pressed/disabled/destructive.
JSON output only.`;

# UI Token Extractor Flow

## Usage
1. `claude-code run ui-token-extractor`
2. Drag screenshots into the image uploader
3. Get 5 Markdown files instantly

## Outputs
- `ui-style-guide.md` ← Your single source of truth
- `accessibility-tokens.md` ← Contrast ratios + tap targets

## Token Naming
Uses primitive + semantic scheme (see prompt-templates.ts)

install_instructions: 2. In Claude Code → Cmd+K → 'Create Flow from JSON'
4. Drag images → Run → Download MD files
